summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the 8‑April‑1985 show with well‑defined bass and crisp Jerry tone
review: Both reviewers rave about the audio fidelity of this pull – the Beyer‑spaced mics deliver a tight low end, clear vocals and Jerry Garcia’s guitar stays bright and present.  No specific musical moments are called out, so the performance itself remains unassessed, but the recording quality is judged to be top‑tier for an ’80s Dead tape.

ai_rating.rationale: High marks for recording quality (5‑star and detailed praise) but no concrete show‑performance data; overall rating reflects excellent audio with unknown musical merit.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Well‑defined bass, crisp Jerry tone, balanced vocals, minimal crowd bleed – described as the best balance among multiple pulls of this date.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia's guitar tone is noted as crisp and well‑balanced; vocals sit nicely in the mix.
